mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 210 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust economic activity, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in > 70% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,500k signals strong consumer confidence and demand, pushing S&P 500 higher over 60 trading days through increased consumption and investment.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1,500k.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% indicates tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of periods where UNRATE remains below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real sales (RSAFS) below 370,000m signals weak consumer spending, reducing corporate revenues and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS falls below 370,000m.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and rises in CPIAUCSL indicate accommodative policy amid inflation, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60d SPX returns in > 70% of cases, or their correlation exceeds 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% signals aggressive monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.
